title: ranunculus auricomus subsp. alpestris
intro: Ranunculus auricomus subsp. alpestris, also known as alpine buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). This perennial plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and it is believed to have medicinal properties due to the presence of ranunculin and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus auricomus subsp. alpestris include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although caution should be exercised due to its toxicity and potential skin irritation.
